title: lichen_l2.c: link_ctx_initialized flag is set before RX callback registration
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:656-680

## Issue
In lichen_l2_iface_init(), the link_ctx_initialized flag is set (line 662) before the RX callback is registered (line 680):

```c
#if HAVE_LICHEN_LINK
    /* Initialize link context before enabling RX */
    lichen_link_init(&link_ctx, eui64);
    atomic_set(&link_ctx_initialized, 1);  // Flag set here
#endif
    ...
    lichen_iface = iface;
    
    /* Register RX callback - must happen AFTER link_ctx is initialized */
    lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback(lora_rx_callback, NULL);  // Callback registered here
```

This ordering is correct for the stated goal (callback must see initialized link_ctx). However, the flag is also used by lichen_l2_enable() re-initialization (line 443) and by RX/TX paths as a guard.

If init fails after setting link_ctx_initialized but before completing (e.g., in the lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64 call at line 683-687), the flag remains set but the interface is in a failed state.

## What goes wrong
- If init fails at goto fail_late_init, line 707 clears link_ctx_initialized
- But if init fails BEFORE fail_late_init but AFTER line 662 (no such code path currently exists), the flag would be left set
- More critically: the iface_init_failed flag (line 710) is the actual error indicator, and TX/RX paths check BOTH flags (line 313, line 350, line 592, line 752)
- The dual-flag system is complex and easy to get wrong in future modifications

## Fix
1. Add a comment at line 662 explaining that fail_late_init cleanup handles this flag on error paths
2. Consider consolidating to a single state flag with enum values (UNINITIALIZED, INITIALIZING, READY, FAILED)
